title: Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku: He Tirohanga Taiao | The Mangakōtukutuku Stream: An Environmental Perspective
subject: Te Reo Māori
culturalContext: Students will explore the environmental health and cultural significance of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, the stream that flows through our college lands and holds deep connections to Ngāti Kahungunu history. The lesson connects to the pūrākau of Kahungunu's journey through this area and the traditional role of the stream as a source of mahinga kai and navigation route.
learningObjectives: Identify and describe at least 5 key Māori environmental terms (e.g., mauri, kaitiakitanga, wai Māori) in the context of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
Conduct a basic mihi and pepeha that incorporates specific reference to Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku and its significance to Ngāti Kahungunu
Create a simple environmental assessment report in te reo Māori using sentence structures and vocabulary appropriate to Year 9 level
Participate in a wānanga discussion using appropriate te reo Māori phrases to express opinions about the stream's health and protection

activities.title: He Mihimihi ki te Awa | Greeting the Stream
activities.description: Students learn and practice a mihi that specifically acknowledges Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, its mauri (life force), and its significance to Ngāti Kahungunu. They will incorporate basic environmental vocabulary and practice pronunciation with support from kaiako and native speakers.

activities.title: He Haerenga Tirotiro | Field Investigation Journey
activities.description: Guided by local kaumātua, students visit three specific sites along Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku (upstream, college boundary, and downstream confluence). They use prepared te reo Māori observation sheets to document water clarity, plant life, and animal presence using Māori environmental terms.

activities.title: He Kōrerorero mō te Kaitiakitanga | Kaitiakitanga Discussion Circle
activities.description: In a whare style setting, students participate in guided discussions using te reo Māori sentence frames to share observations and opinions about the stream's health. They discuss traditional and modern kaitiakitanga practices specific to waterways in Ngāti Kahungunu rohe.

activities.title: He Ripoata Taiao | Environmental Reporting
activities.description: Working in small groups, students create simple visual reports (posters or digital slides) using te reo Māori to present their findings. They include basic data, observations, and recommendations for protecting the mauri of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku using appropriate sentence structures.

resources: Ngāti Kahungunu Iwi Incorporated environmental management plans and te reo resources
Local kaumātua and kaitiaki from Ngāti Kahungunu with knowledge of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
Te Aka Māori Dictionary online and mobile application
Hawke's Bay Regional Council monitoring data for Mangakōtukutuku Stream

tasks: Oral presentation of mihi incorporating stream-specific references
Completed te reo Māori field observation sheets
Participation in kōrerorero using appropriate te reo phrases
Group environmental report in te reo Māori with basic recommendations

nzcAlignment: Te Reo Māori: Communication - Level 4: Use basic te reo Māori patterns, structures, and conventions to communicate information and ideas
Te Reo Māori: Language Knowledge - Level 4: Understand how te reo Māori reflects and expresses Māori culture
Social Sciences: Place and Environment - Level 4: Understand how people's management of resources impacts on environmental and social sustainability
Science: Living World - Level 4: Explore how the natural environment is represented and the impact of human activity on it
